metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4703
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1: Rock1 partially co-localizes with presynaptic marker synaptophysin in immunofluorescence stainings. The imaging resolution does not enable a more specific annotation than "synapse".

From result section: "Double immunolabeling with antibodies against ROCKα and the motoneuronal marker SMI32 further confirmed that this isoform was expressed in HMN cell bodies (Fig. 1A, inset). Inspection of ROCKα immunostaining at higher magnification also revealed the presence of ROCKα-immunoreactive (ir) puncta-like structures in the HN, which in some cases colocalized with the synaptic marker syn (Fig. 1B). On the other hand, ROCKβ labeling consisted of punctate staining densely distributed throughout the nucleus (Fig. 1C), in some examples delineating neuron-like profiles, and around SMI32-identified HMNs (Fig. 1C, inset). ROCKβ-ir punctate also partially colocalized with syn (Fig. 1D). Our quantitative analysis revealed that 7.4 ± 0.9% (n = 65 HMNs) of syn-ir puncta apposed to the perikarya of HMNs showed ROCKβ-ir, whereas only 0.5 ± 0.2% (n = 55 HMNs) coexpressed ROCKα. These data suggest that ROCKα is the main isoform expressed in HMNs, whereas ROCKβ is the predominant isoform expressed in synaptic structures."

Note; Rock1 = Rock beta, Rock2 = Rock alpha (see also PMID: 20803696, figure 1). Experimental description: Rock naming is somewhat confusing; Rock1 = Rock beta, Rock2 = Rock alpha. Etymology from PMID: 20803696

MYM: "Wistar rats of either sex were obtained from an authorized supplier"

antibody: anti-ROCKβ (1:50; Santa Cruz Biotechnology)
